category_sources: source_facts.description says command-line client for Splitgraph, a version control system for data; official Splitgraph docs describe sgr as manipulating data images like code repositories: https://www.splitgraph.com/docs/sgr-advanced/getting-started/introduction
display_name_sources: source_facts.name is sgr; official Splitgraph docs use sgr as the CLI name: https://www.splitgraph.com/docs/sgr-cli/introduction
docs_sources: Official Splitgraph docs provide advanced sgr docs, CLI introduction, and Python commandline API reference: https://www.splitgraph.com/docs/sgr-cli/introduction
repo_sources: Official Splitgraph/PyPI package metadata and documentation identify Splitgraph Core as containing the sgr CLI and reference cloning https://github.com/splitgraph/splitgraph.git.
tags_sources: source_facts.description and official Splitgraph docs describe sgr as a Splitgraph CLI for versioning/querying data images on PostgreSQL: https://www.splitgraph.com/docs/sgr-advanced/getting-started/introduction
